for.p: A person (A) who has the right to dispose of premises

ul.li: (a) as to the terms on which A offers to dispose of the premises to B;
(b) by not disposing of the premises to B;
(c) in A's treatment of B with respect to things done in relation to persons seeking premises

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: a particular person who is a party to the disposal of a commonhold unit

to.p: discriminate against another person by not being a party to the disposal

condition.p: an interest in the commonhold unit cannot be disposed of without that particular person
this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: A person who has the right to dispose of premises

to.p: in connection with anything done in relation to their occupation or disposal, harass—

ul.li: (a) a person who occupies them;
(b) a person who applies for them

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: A person (A) who has the right to dispose of premises

ul.li: (a) as to the terms on which A offers to dispose of the premises to B;
(b) by not disposing of the premises to B;
(c) in A's treatment of B with respect to things done in relation to persons seeking premises

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: a particular person who is a party to the disposal of a commonhold unit

to.p: victimise another person by not being a party to the disposal

condition.p: an interest in the commonhold unit cannot be disposed of without that particular person
this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: A person whose permission is required for the disposal of premises

to.p: discriminate against another by not giving permission for the disposal of the premises to the other

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: A person whose permission is required for the disposal of premises

to.p: in relation to an application for permission to dispose of the premises, harass a person—

ul.li: (a) who applies for permission to dispose of the premises,
or (b) to whom the disposal would be made if permission were given

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: A person whose permission is required for the disposal of premises

to.p: victimise another by not giving permission for the disposal of the premises to the other

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: discriminate against a person (B) who occupies the premises—

ul.li: (a) in the way in which A allows B,
or by not allowing B,
to make use of a benefit or facility;
(b) by evicting B (or taking steps for the purpose of securing B's eviction);
(c) by subjecting B to any other detriment

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: in relation to their management, harass—

ul.li: (a) a person who occupies them;
(b) a person who applies for them

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: victimise a person (B) who occupies the premises—

ul.li: (a) in the way in which A allows B,
or by not allowing B,
to make use of a benefit or facility;
(b) by evicting B (or taking steps for the purpose of securing B's eviction);
(c) by subjecting B to any other detriment

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule
(a) a controller of let premises

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: provide by regulations that a disabled person is entitled to make relevant adjustments to common parts in relation to premises in Scotland

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: consult a Minister of the Crown

condition.p: making regulations under subsection (1)
this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule
(a) prescribe things which are,
or which are not,
to be treated as relevant adjustments;
(b) prescribe circumstances in which the consent of an owner of the common parts is required before a disabled person may make an adjustment;
(c) provide that the consent to adjustments is not to be withheld unreasonably;
(d) prescribe matters to be taken into account,
or to be disregarded,
in deciding whether it is reasonable to consent to adjustments;
(e) prescribe circumstances in which consent to adjustments is to be taken to be withheld;
(f) make provision about the imposition of conditions on consent to adjustments;
(g) make provision as to circumstances in which the sheriff may make an order authorising a disabled person to carry out adjustments;
(h) make provision about the responsibility for costs arising (directly or indirectly) from an adjustment;
(i) make provision about the reinstatement of the common parts to the condition they were in before an adjustment was made;
(j) make provision about the giving of notice to the owners of the common parts and other persons;
(k) make provision about agreements between a disabled person and an owner of the common parts;

(l) make provision about the registration of information in the Land Register of Scotland or the recording of documents in the Register of Sasines relating to an entitlement of a disabled person or an obligation on an owner of the common parts;
(m) make provision about the effect of such registration or recording;
(n) make provision about who is to be treated as being,
or as not being,
a person entitled to occupy premises otherwise than as tenant or owner.

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age (b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to the provision of accommodation if the provision—

ul.li: (a) is generally for the purpose of short stays by individuals who live elsewhere,
or (b) is for the purpose only of exercising a public function or providing a service to the public or a section of the public.

condition.p: this applies to—

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule
